name: when-using-advanced-swarm-use-swarm-advanced
description: Advanced swarm patterns with dynamic topology switching and self-organizing behaviors for complex multi-agent coordination
capabilities: Initialize swarms with hierarchical, mesh, star, ring, or adaptive topology patterns
Spawn and coordinate specialized agents including hierarchical-coordinator, mesh-coordinator, and adaptive-coordinator
Monitor real-time performance metrics and identify bottlenecks across the swarm
Apply dynamic optimizations including topology switching, agent rebalancing, and resource allocation
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown SOP files. No executable code, no file system access, no network calls, and no code execution paths detected. Pure workflow documentation for Claude Flow swarm coordination. All 133 static findings are false positives caused by pattern-matching on bash examples in documentation code blocks.
valueStatement: Complex multi-agent projects require sophisticated coordination. This skill provides systematic patterns for dynamic topology switching, self-organizing agent behaviors, and adaptive resource allocation to optimize swarm performance.
userTitle: Deploy advanced multi-agent swarms with dynamic coordination
limitations: Requires Claude Flow installed and configured to execute commands
Cannot create or modify actual agent code without Claude Flow MCP
Performance improvements depend on workload characteristics and system resources
Does not replace human oversight for security and quality decisions

useCases.title: Scale Multi-Agent Development
useCases.description: Coordinate large development teams using hierarchical task delegation patterns with performance monitoring

useCases.title: Automate Infrastructure Scaling
useCases.description: Deploy adaptive swarms that automatically switch topologies based on workload demands and resource availability

useCases.title: Experiment with Coordination
useCases.description: Test different topology patterns and self-organizing behaviors in controlled multi-agent experiments

promptTemplates.title: Basic Swarm Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Use swarm-advanced to initialize a hierarchical swarm with 10 agents. Configure memory coordination and verify initialization.

promptTemplates.title: Mesh Coordination
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy a mesh topology swarm for collaborative analysis. Spawn researcher and analyst agents with peer coordination enabled.

promptTemplates.title: Adaptive Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Monitor the current swarm performance. Identify bottlenecks and apply dynamic optimizations including topology switching if needed.

promptTemplates.title: Complete Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute the complete advanced swarm workflow: initialize with adaptive topology, deploy 8 specialized agents, monitor for 5 minutes, then optimize and generate a performance report.

bestPractices: Start with hierarchical or star topology for simpler workflows before advancing to mesh or adaptive patterns
Establish performance baselines before applying optimizations to measure improvement accurately
Test topology switches in non-production environments first to validate behavior
antiPatterns: Avoid switching topologies without draining active tasks first
Do not exceed recommended agent counts without verifying system resources
Avoid operating without continuous monitoring during optimization phases

faq.question: Which topologies work best for different project types?
faq.answer: Hierarchical suits task delegation, mesh enables peer collaboration, star works for simple workflows, ring handles sequential processing, and adaptive optimizes variable workloads automatically.

faq.question: What are the recommended agent limits?
faq.answer: Hierarchical supports 10 agents, mesh handles 8, and adaptive accommodates 12 agents. Scale gradually while monitoring system resource consumption.

faq.question: How does this integrate with other Claude Flow skills?
faq.answer: Swarm-advanced integrates with swarm-orchestration for complex tasks, performance-analysis for insights, and hive-mind for collective intelligence patterns.

faq.question: Is my data safe during swarm operations?
faq.answer: This skill only coordinates agents you control. All data flows within your environment. Review agent permissions before deployment and use memory coordination only for non-sensitive configuration data.

faq.question: Why is performance not improving after optimization?
faq.answer: Verify baseline metrics are accurate. Check if bottlenecks were correctly identified. Try different optimization strategies or adjust topology parameters. Some workloads may have reached natural limits.

faq.question: How does adaptive topology differ from manual switching?
faq.answer: Adaptive topology automatically monitors workloads and switches patterns based on thresholds you configure. Manual switching gives you full control but requires active monitoring and intervention.
